satellites.name: Hubble Space Telescope
satellites.description: The Hubble Space Telescope is a space telescope that was launched into low Earth orbit in 1990 and remains in operation. It is one of NASA's Great Observatories, providing deep and clear views of the universe.

satellites.name: International Space Station
satellites.description: The International Space Station is a modular space station in low Earth orbit. It is a multinational collaborative project involving five participating space agencies.

satellites.name: Starlink-1007
satellites.description: Starlink is a satellite internet constellation operated by SpaceX, providing high-speed internet access to underserved areas around the world.

satellites.name: GPS IIF-12
satellites.description: GPS IIF-12 is a United States navigation satellite which forms part of the Global Positioning System. It was the twelfth and final IIF series satellite.

satellites.name: Sentinel-2A
satellites.description: Sentinel-2A is a European Earth observation satellite that provides high-resolution optical imagery for land monitoring, emergency response, and climate change research.

satellites.name: James Webb Space Telescope
satellites.description: The James Webb Space Telescope is a space telescope designed to conduct infrared astronomy. It is the largest optical telescope in space.

satellites.name: GOES-18
satellites.description: GOES-18 is a weather satellite operated by NOAA, providing continuous monitoring of weather conditions across the western hemisphere.

satellites.name: Intelsat 37e
satellites.description: Intelsat 37e is a communications satellite providing high-throughput broadband services to the Americas, Europe, and Africa.

satellites.name: Iridium-150
satellites.description: Iridium NEXT is a constellation of 66 low-Earth orbit satellites providing global satellite phone and data services.

satellites.name: TerraSAR-X
satellites.description: TerraSAR-X is a German Earth observation satellite using synthetic aperture radar to provide high-resolution images for scientific and commercial purposes.

satellites.name: Voyager 1
satellites.description: Voyager 1 is a space probe launched by NASA to study the outer Solar System and interstellar space. It is the most distant human-made object from Earth.

satellites.name: Landsat 9
satellites.description: Landsat 9 is an Earth observation satellite that continues the Landsat program's 50-year legacy of monitoring land use, natural resources, and environmental changes.

satellites.name: Galileo-27
satellites.description: Galileo is Europe's global navigation satellite system, providing high-precision positioning services to users worldwide.

satellites.name: NOAA-20
satellites.description: NOAA-20 is a weather satellite that provides data for weather forecasting, climate monitoring, and atmospheric science research.

satellites.name: Artemis I Orion
satellites.description: Orion is a spacecraft built for deep space missions. Artemis I was an uncrewed test flight that successfully completed a lunar orbit mission.

satellites.name: Sputnik 1
satellites.description: Sputnik 1 was the first artificial satellite to orbit Earth. Its successful launch marked the beginning of the Space Age.

satellites.name: ISS-1 Zarya
satellites.description: Zarya is the first module of the International Space Station, providing propulsion and power to the station during its early construction.

satellites.name: ALOS-2
satellites.description: ALOS-2 is a Japanese Earth observation satellite using L-band synthetic aperture radar to monitor disasters, forestry, and environmental changes.

satellites.name: K2-12
satellites.description: K2-12 is a South Korean Earth observation satellite providing high-resolution imagery for agricultural monitoring and urban planning.

satellites.name: Boeing X-37B
satellites.description: The X-37B is an autonomous spaceplane operated by the U.S. Space Force, conducting long-duration orbital missions with classified payloads.
